signals.reasoning: The live SOL short is still a meaningful loser after fully round-tripping from a real winner, retains none of peak PnL, still has no stop, and liquidation distance is still under 1%. Even with only a CAUTION event brake, this remains a cleanup exit rather than a valid hold.
BTC remains in a broad recovery tape and does not offer a cleaner immediate action than simply flattening the failed SOL short.
ETH is also participating in the same rebound tape, so it is not where disciplined attention should go while an urgent failed short is still open.
MERL is constructive, but the rebound is already far enough along that it is not a cleaner next decision than flattening the SOL risk.
ORDI has regained strength, but it is already extended enough that it belongs on a watchlist rather than as a fresh move right after a failed short.
STABLE has one of the strongest squeeze setups on the board, but the immediate task here is still position cleanup, not rotating into a fresh long under a live macro caution regime.
HMSTR is too thin and too low-quality structurally to justify fresh attention in a position-management cycle.
BOME is just another noisy rebound name and not a better immediate call than standing down after closing SOL.
TST remains far too extended and crowded to trust as a disciplined fresh idea.
WCT has bounce momentum, but the setup is too far along and too mixed to treat as a better action than simply flattening failed risk.
